It is recommended that righteous Muslims who knew the deceased well, and were among his or her neighbours, should testify to others about the good qualities of the deceased. This is a means of reminding others of righteousness and spurring them on to good deeds. The Prophet (r) personally encouraged this practice and pointed out that such praise is of benefit to the dead person. 51 Porridge made from flour, bran and honey. 52 Sahih Al Al-Al-Al-Al-Bukhari, vol.7, p.401, no.593 and Sahih Muslim, vol.4, p.1202, no.5491. 53 вҖҳAbdullaah ibn Abee Bakr was one of the later narrators of this hadith. 54 People later elaborated on this practice until it became an exaggerated ritual with much pomp and show. Later scholars considered its distorted form an innovation and abandoned it. 55 Sunan Ibn-i-Majah, vol.2, p.455, no.1610 and authenticated in sahih Sunan Ibn Maajah, vol.1, pp.268-9, no.1307.

Abul-Aswad related that he came to Madeenah during the time of an epidemic and he sat next to вҖҳUmar ibn al-Khattaab. When a bier passed by, the people praised the person and вҖҳUmar said, вҖңIt has been affirmed.вҖқ Another bier passed by and the people praised the person and вҖҳUmar said, вҖңIt has been affirmed.вҖқ When a third bier passed by, the people spoke badly of the deceased and вҖҳUmar said, вҖңIt has been affirmed.вҖқ Abul-Aswad asked him what was affirmed and he replied, вҖңI said what the Prophet (r) said: вҖңAllaah will put in paradise any Muslim for whom four have testified to his goodness.вҖқ56 пәҚо Ё пәғЩҺп»Ёп№¶пәұЩҚ пәғЩҺп»Ҙо ўп»Ҙо  п»Ӣ о Ёп»ҹп»Ёпұ пә’Щҗп»І r п»—п№¶пәҺЩ„ЩҺ : )) пәҚп»ЁЩҗп»ӘЩҗ о   пә Щҗп»ҙпәӯо ўпәҺпә•ЩҚ п»ӨЩҗп»Ҙо  п»ҙо ўЩ„ЩҸ пәғЩҺпә’о ўпә”п№І пәғЩҺп»«о  п»Ңо  пә’о ў пәғЩҺпәӯо Ў п»ҹп№¶п»Әо Ўпә©о  пәёп№ҫп»¬о  п»ӯпә•п№ё п»“п№¶п»ҙо Ўп»Өо  п» Щҗп»ЎЩҚ п»ҙо ўпәҙо Ў п»Өо ўпәҺ п»ӨЩҗп»Ҙо  п»Ө о  п»ӯп»Ҙо Ўп» п№¶п»Өо ўпәҺ п»»п№¶ пәҳп№¶п»Ңо   п»Өо Ўпә•п№ё п»ҹп№¶п»Әо ўп»Ҹп№¶п»”п№¶пәӯо   п»“Щҗп»ҙп»ӘЩҗ п»ӯо ўп»ңп№ёп»Ўо   п»—п№¶пә’Щҗп» п№ҫпә•п№ё п»“Щҗп»ҙп»ӘЩҗ п»ӢЩҗп» п№ҫп»Өо ў пәҮЩҗп»»пұ  п»—п№¶пәҺЩ„ЩҺ п»—п№¶пә©о  п»Ҙо ўп»Ёп№¶п»ҙо ўпәҚп№ҫп»·ЩҺпә© ((. Anas also related that the Prophet said, вҖңFor any Muslim who dies and four of his close neighbours testify that they have known him to be good, Allaah - the Blessed, the Highest - will say, вҖңI have accepted your testimony and forgiven him for what you do not know about.вҖқ57 The practice of requesting witnesses after the burial prayer and the bearing of witness by those who do not even know the deceased is bidвҖҳah and futile, for false witness will never benefit anyone in the next life. 56 Collected by Sahih Al-Al-Al-Al-Al-Bukhari, vol.2, pp.253-54, no.449, Mishkat Al-Masabih,, vol.1, p.350 atTirmithee and an-NasaaвҖҷee. 57 Collected by Ahmad (sahih) See Ahkaamul Janaaiz, pp.45-6.

CHAPTER THREE: PREPARING THE BODY There should be no delay in the washing, shrouding and burial of the individual once he or she has died, as was mentioned earlier and supported by the ProphetвҖҷs instruction, вҖңHasten the funeral rites.вҖқ1 A. Injunctions Concerning Washing Washing the dead body prior to shrouding and burial is obligatory, according to numerous recorded instructions given by the Prophet (r). For example, when his daughter, Zaynab, passed away, he told the women who were preparing her for burial: )) пәҺо  пәҚп»Ҹп№ҫпәҙЩҗп» п№ҫп»Ёп№¶п»¬ (( вҖң You should wash her...вҖқ2 This obligation is however considered Fard Kifaayah (a collective responsibility), if there is no one to claim the body. If someone performs the washing, the community is freed from the obligation, but if no one does it, the whole community is considered sinful. As a general rule, males should take the responsibility of washing males, and females should wash females. This rule is to avoid the arousal of any undesirable feelings during this delicate procedure. The only exception to the rule is in the case of husband and wife, or small children. Permission for husbands to wash their wives and visa versa is based primarily on the fact that 1 Collected in all of the six books of hadith. Sahih Al-Al-Al-Al-Al-Bukhari, vol.2, p.225, no.401, Sahih Muslim, vol.2, p.448, no.2059, Sunan Abu Dawud, vol.2, pp.897-8, no. 3153, Sunan Ibn -i- Majah, vol.2, p.382, no.1477 and Mishkat Al-Masabih, vol.1, p.338. 2 Reported by Umm вҖҳAteeyah and in all the six books of hadith. Sahih Al-Al-Al-Al-Al-Bukhari, vol.2, p.194, no.344, Sahih Muslim, vol.2, p.445, no.2041, Sunan Abu Dawud vol.2, p.894, no.3136, Sunan Ibn-i-Majah, vol.2, p.372, no.1458, Muwatta, p.105, no.514 and Mishkat Al-Masabih, vol.1, p.344. 2

there is no prohibition from doing so. It is also based on statements of the Prophet (r) and his wives. Such was the case of those who took charge of washing the Prophet (r). So he said, вҖңMay my father be a ransom for you. You are the most pure. You were clean and pure in life and you are clean and pure after your demise.вҖқ6 If relatives are not available, it is recommended that those who wash the body be among the pious, due to the delicate nature of this procedure. Those who deal with the dead in hospitals and morgues, usually become quite callous and rough or disrespectful in their treatment of the corpses. The same is true of those hired to wash the dead. Consequently, God-fearing individuals should be chosen in order to ensure proper treatment of the dead. CHAPTER FOUR: FUNERAL PRAYER A. Injunctions Concerning the Prayer The performance of Salaah al-Janaazah is considered Fard Kifaayah (a communal duty). If someone is buried without it being performed, the whole community is held responsible, but as long as some gather and perform it, the obligation is removed from the community as a whole. EXCEPTIONS A Child Below the Age of Puberty: A child born dead or aborted after the fourth month, or one that dies before reaching puberty, does not have to have a funeral prayer because they have been guaranteed paradise by Allaah and His Prophet (r). Jaabir Ibn Samurah reported that when a man got very sick and his family began screaming, his neighbour went to the Prophet (r) and told him that the man had died. The Prophet (r) asked him what made him think so and he replied that he saw him. The Prophet (r) then told him that he was not yet dead. When he returned, the manвҖҷs family again screamed and his wife told the neighbour to go and inform the Prophet (r). The neighbour asked Allaah to curse the man and then went to look at him. He found, that he killed himself with a broad blade, so he went and informed the Prophet (r) that he was dead. When the Prophet (r) asked him how he knew, he described how he found him. The Prophet (r) then asked the neighbour if he saw the dead man with his own eyes and when he replied that he had, the Prophet (r) then said, вҖңIn that case, I wonвҖҷt make salaah for him.вҖқ10 Debtors: In the beginning, the Prophet (r) refused to lead the funeral prayer for those who died leaving behind debts and no means to pay them. When the bier of Umm Kulthoom bint вҖҳAlee30 and her son Zayd was brought together and the youth was placed behind the Imaam, he disputed it and looked to Ibn вҖҳAbbaas, 28 This addition found in Sahih Al Al-Al-Al-Al-Bukhari, vol.5, pp.413-4, no.595. 29 Sunan Abu Dawud,vol.1, p.155, no.587 and authenticated in sahih Sunan Abee Daawood, vol.1, p.117, no.548. 30 Also the wife of вҖҳUmar ibn al-Khattaab. Aboo Hurayrah, Aboo SaвҖҳeed and Aboo Qataadah and asked, вҖңWhat is this?вҖқ They replied, вҖңIt is the Sunnah.вҖқ31 It is also allowable that Salaatul-Janaazah be performed on each person individually as this was also done on occasion by the Prophet (r) and it is the original practice. Ibn вҖҳAbbaas said: When AllaahвҖҷs Messenger came accross Hamzah, he asked for him and his body was prepared and placed in the direction of Makkah. He then prayed the funeral prayer using nine takbeers. Then the bodies of the other martyrs were brought. Each martyrвҖҷs body was placed beside HamzahвҖҷs body, one at a time, and he prayed for each along with HamzahвҖҷs body until he completed seventy-two funeral prayers.32 C. Location of the Prayer According to the practice of the Prophet (r), there are two locations where the funeral prayer may be held; inside the masjid or outside the masjid at a prayer place reserved for it.

Dianjurkan bagi umat Muslim yang saleh, yang mengenal almarhum dengan baik dan termasuk tetangganya, untuk bersaksi kepada orang lain tentang kebaikan-kebaikan almarhum. Hal ini merupakan sarana untuk mengingatkan orang lain tentang kebenaran dan mendorong mereka kepada amal kebaikan. Nabi п·ә secara pribadi mendorong praktik ini dan menunjukkan bahwa pujian semacam itu bermanfaat bagi orang yang telah meninggal. Abul-Aswad menceritakan bahwa ketika tiga jenazah melintas di hadapan Umar bin al-Khattab вҖ” satu dipuji, satu lagi dipuji, dan yang ketiga dicela вҖ” Umar berkata, "Aku mengatakan apa yang Nabi п·ә katakan: Allah akan memasukkan ke dalam surga setiap Muslim yang empat orang bersaksi tentang kebaikannya." Anas juga meriwayatkan bahwa Nabi п·ә bersabda, "Bagi setiap Muslim yang meninggal dan empat tetangga terdekatnya bersaksi bahwa mereka mengenal kebaikannya, Allah Yang Mahamulia dan Mahatinggi akan berfirman: Aku menerima kesaksian kalian dan mengampuninya atas apa yang tidak kalian ketahui tentang dirinya."

BAB TIGA: MEMPERSIAPKAN JENAZAH вҖ” Tidak boleh ada penundaan dalam memandikan, mengafani, dan menguburkan seseorang setelah meninggal, sebagaimana disebutkan sebelumnya dan didukung oleh perintah Nabi п·ә, "Percepatlah pengurusan jenazah." Memandikan jenazah sebelum dikafani dan dikubur adalah wajib berdasarkan banyak instruksi yang tercatat dari Nabi п·ә. Misalnya, ketika putrinya Zaynab wafat, beliau berkata kepada wanita-wanita yang mempersiapkan pemakamannya: "Mandikanlah ia." Kewajiban ini dianggap fardhu kifayah, jika tidak ada yang mengurus jenazah. Jika seseorang melakukan pemandian, komunitas terbebas dari kewajiban, tetapi jika tidak ada yang melakukannya, seluruh komunitas dianggap berdosa. Sebagai aturan umum, laki-laki bertanggung jawab memandikan laki-laki, dan perempuan memandikan perempuan. Aturan ini untuk menghindari timbulnya perasaan yang tidak diinginkan selama prosedur yang peka ini. Satu-satunya pengecualian adalah dalam kasus suami dan istri, atau anak kecil.

Aisyah melaporkan bahwa Nabi п·ә bersabda kepadanya, "Jika engkau meninggal sebelumku, aku akan memandikanmu, mengafanimu, mensalatimu, dan menguburkanmu." Aisyah juga berkata, "Seandainya aku mengetahui lebih awal apa yang aku ketahui kemudian, hanya istri-istri Nabi п·ә yang boleh memandikan beliau." Mereka yang bertanggung jawab memandikan jenazah hendaknya adalah yang paling berpengetahuan tentang tata caranya, sebaiknya dari antara anggota keluarga atau kerabat dekat almarhum. Begitulah yang terjadi pada mereka yang bertanggung jawab memandikan Nabi п·ә, yaitu para sepupu beliau: Ali, al-Fadl bin Abbas, dan Usamah putra Zaid bin Haritsah, anak angkat Nabi п·ә.

Tata cara memandikan: Pertama, jenazah dibaringkan, pakaiannya dilepas, dan sendinya dilonggarkan jika memungkinkan. Kain harus ditempatkan menutupi aurat. Perut ditekan untuk mengeluarkan kotoran yang tersisa. Kemudian gunakan kain basah untuk memandikan jasad, dimulai dari bagian kanan sebagaimana dalam wudu. Setelah selesai wudu, rambut wanita diuraikan jika dalam keadaan dikepang dan dicuci dengan bersih. Kemudian sisa tubuh dimandikan dengan membalik jenazah ke samping agar dapat menyelesaikan sisi kanan sebelum sisi kiri. Jasad hendaknya dimandikan minimal tiga kali dengan air yang mengandung bahan pembersih seperti sabun atau desinfektan. Pemandian terakhir hendaknya mengandung wewangian seperti kapur barus. Setelahnya, jenazah dikeringkan dan rambutnya disisir. Bagi wanita, rambutnya dikepang tiga; satu dari bagian depan dan dua dari samping, lalu diletakkan di belakang kepalanya. Bagi jenazah yang wafat dalam keadaan ihram, tidak boleh menggunakan wewangian dan kepala tidak boleh ditutup, karena Allah akan membangkitkannya pada Hari Kiamat dalam keadaan bertalbiyah. Bagi yang gugur sebagai syahid, jasadnya tidak dimandikan sama sekali, melainkan dikubur sebagaimana adanya.

Mengenai kafan: Mengafani jenazah adalah wajib berdasarkan perintah Nabi п·ә. Warna yang lebih baik adalah putih, karena Ibnu Abbas mengutip Rasulullah п·ә bersabda, "Kenakanlah pakaian putih, karena itu adalah sebaik-baik pakaian kalian, dan kafanilah pula orang-orang yang meninggal di antara kalian dengannya." Dianjurkan juga agar kain kafan atau setidaknya salah satu lembarnya berwarna bergaris. Jabir menceritakan bahwa Nabi п·ә bersabda, "Jika salah seorang dari kalian meninggal dan tersedia kain, hendaklah ia dikafani dengan kain bergaris." Dianjurkan pula untuk mengasapi kafan dengan dupa sebanyak tiga kali, kecuali bagi yang berstatus muhrim. Tidak ada perbedaan antara laki-laki dan perempuan dalam jumlah lembar kain kafan.

Mengenai prosesi jenazah: Abu Hurairah mengutip Nabi п·ә bersabda, "Segerakanlah prosesi jenazah; jika almarhum adalah orang yang saleh, maka tempat yang kalian bawa kepadanya adalah kebaikan baginya; dan jika ia bukan orang yang saleh, maka kalian meletakkan keburukan dari pundak kalian." Pejalan kaki boleh berjalan di belakang, di depan, di kanan, maupun di kiri keranda, selama tetap berada dekat dengannya. Pengendara hendaknya berada di belakang keranda. Bagi wanita, tidak dianjurkan mengikuti prosesi jenazah meskipun tidak dilarang secara mutlak.

BAB EMPAT: SALAT JENAZAH вҖ” Pelaksanaan salat jenazah dianggap fardhu kifayah. Jika seseorang dikubur tanpa dilaksanakan salat jenazah, seluruh komunitas menanggung dosa, tetapi jika sebagian orang berkumpul dan melaksanakannya, kewajiban itu gugur dari komunitas secara keseluruhan. Pengecualian meliputi anak yang belum balig, jenazah orang kafir, dan orang munafik. Salat jenazah harus dilaksanakan berjamaah. Dianjurkan agar mereka yang berada di belakang imam membentuk setidaknya tiga shaf. Imam atau wakilnya lebih berhak memimpin salat jenazah daripada wali almarhum. Jika keduanya tidak hadir, yang paling banyak hafalan Al-Qurannya yang memimpin salat.

Mengenai lokasi salat jenazah: menurut praktik Nabi п·ә, ada dua lokasi yang diperbolehkan, yaitu di dalam masjid atau di luar masjid di tempat khusus yang disediakan untuk itu. Tidak boleh melaksanakan salat jenazah di antara kubur-kubur.
